programme.title: The Blue Realm Ep110: The Ship Sinkers
programme.desc: Mothballed, de-commissioned naval vessels are toxic time-bombs, filled with PCB’s, asbestos, lead paint, oil and grease. Hundreds rust in dockyards around the world. What can be done with them? A team of Canadians has developed an innovative solution…they clean the ships...then sink them!

programme.title: Journeys In Africa Ep206: Victoria Falls…The Smoke That Thunders
programme.desc: One of the 7 Natural Wonders of the World, Victoria Falls is an amazing spectacle that straddles the Zambia/Zimbabwe border. The force of the water is so intense that the water bouncing back creates a rainforest. Remarkably, the wildlife in the area is as abundant as the water going over the falls.

programme.title: All Stars
programme.desc: With a successful mission under their belts, the team of All Stars can relax and enjoy a day of diving.. or so they thought. The CIA is calling the team back into action for a VBSS mission to secure deadly poisons that are inbound for civilians. If the team thought the previous mission was challenging, this one makes it look like a cake walk. The All Stars must call upon all their training thus far to make it through this mission, when an unknown element threatens to jeopardize it and them.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Season 3 of Surviving Mann starts with no time wasted... straight to PT. The contestants are thrown straight into physical training, to determine where everyone lies and how to properly group them into the iconic Black and Blue Surviving Mann teams. From Olympic athletes to cast favorites, these eight competitors are ready to prove what they’ve got.

programme.title: Aftermath
programme.desc: With the first episode of season three of Surviving Mann in the books, it’s time to dissect and analyze. Host Chris Heaven kicks off episode one of Aftermath by taking a look into the contestants' mental headspace during the grueling first day. Contestant and three-time olympian Lanny Barnes recounts the first day under Coach Mann and how it stacks up to his training in the past.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Mike Glover, Green Beret and retired CIA operative, joins Don to kick it up a notch for our contestants. What would Surviving Mann be without some log PT? If it’s good enough for the Seals - it’s good enough for our contestants. If that wasn’t hard enough, Don and Mike have the contestants do some tire pulling, a run, tug of war, land nav and a Tyrolean traverse over alligator infested water. Oh, and did we mention the 40mph wind and rain storm?

programme.title: Aftermath
programme.desc: Aftermath hosts Chris and Cindy sit down with contestants Caleb and Jen to discuss one of the hardest days in Surviving Mann history. Caleb gives insight on his tug of war strategy.. which took inspiration from an unusual source. Next, Dr. Jeff West gives us the scoop about his life changing product. In the same vein, Eric from Nutrient Survival goes over what his company is doing for soldiers and civilians alike.

programme.desc: The wildest, wackiest, and most jaw-dropping videos. From dangerous severe weather, to heart-stopping close calls and adorable animal antics - all caught on camera.

programme.title: Science on the Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Cousteau dives off the coast of Australia to explore the Great Barrier Reef. He then heads to James Cook University to get an up-close look at some of the country's deadliest creatures. Philippe ends his journey by visiting the National Sea Simulator at the Australian Institute of Marine Science.

programme.title: Science on the Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Cousteau dives off the coast of Australia to explore the Great Barrier Reef. He then heads to James Cook University to get an up-close look at some of the country's deadliest creatures. Philippe ends his journey by visiting the National Sea Simulator at the Australian Institute of Marine Science.

programme.title: Science on the Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Cousteau dives off the coast of Australia to explore the Great Barrier Reef. He then heads to James Cook University to get an up-close look at some of the country's deadliest creatures. Philippe ends his journey by visiting the National Sea Simulator at the Australian Institute of Marine Science.

programme.title: Science on the Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Cousteau dives off the coast of Australia to explore the Great Barrier Reef. He then heads to James Cook University to get an up-close look at some of the country's deadliest creatures. Philippe ends his journey by visiting the National Sea Simulator at the Australian Institute of Marine Science.

programme.title: Science on the Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Cousteau dives off the coast of Australia to explore the Great Barrier Reef. He then heads to James Cook University to get an up-close look at some of the country's deadliest creatures. Philippe ends his journey by visiting the National Sea Simulator at the Australian Institute of Marine Science.

programme.title: The Week As It Happened
programme.desc: Get caught up on all the big stories and breaking news from the week.

programme.title: Caught On Clips
programme.desc: The wildest, wackiest, and most jaw-dropping videos. From dangerous severe weather, to heart-stopping close calls and adorable animal antics - all caught on camera.

programme.title: Science on the Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Cousteau dives off the coast of Australia to explore the Great Barrier Reef. He then heads to James Cook University to get an up-close look at some of the country's deadliest creatures. Philippe ends his journey by visiting the National Sea Simulator at the Australian Institute of Marine Science.

programme.title: St. Augustine
programme.desc: Explore the Spanish architecture of St. Augustine, Florida with Jefferson Graham of Photowalks. Learn tips for taking photographs in the beautiful sunshine.

programme.title: Charleston
programme.desc: Jefferson Graham of Photowalks takes you to one of the richest and most populous colonies in the pre-Revolutionary War era: Charleston, South Carolina. Explore Charleston's layered history and scenic waterfront, along with its picturesque cobblestone streets.
